3.label: Developing
3.description: Wants to improve. Lacks direction or resource.

4.label: On Track
4.description: Core confidence. Specific targeted gaps remain.

staffCapability.label: Staff Digital Capability & Confidence
subCriteria: Staff self-reported digital confidence
Observed competence in core tools (Teams, M365, VLE)
Willingness to try new digital approaches
Engagement with CPD opportunities
Peer-to-peer digital sharing and modelling

levels.1: Staff openly resistant or unable to use basic digital tools. No CPD engagement. Low confidence is a barrier to delivery.
levels.2: Inconsistent confidence across team. Some staff managing; others significantly behind. CPD happening but not embedded.
levels.3: Most staff functional with core tools. Motivation to improve present but CPD not yet systematic or evidenced.
levels.4: Staff generally confident. Team engages with CPD. A few stretching into innovation. Gaps narrow and targeted.
levels.5: High-confidence team. Staff model good digital practice, share resources, and can support colleagues independently.

hoaLeadership.label: HoA Leadership, Planning & Strategy
subCriteria: HoA digital vision for their area
Digital planning in curriculum and BP
HoA engagement with DPC coaching
Digital agenda in team meetings
Response to audit findings and AFIs

levels.1: HoA disengaged or unaware of digital agenda. No planning. Digital not on team agenda.
levels.2: HoA willing but reactive. No proactive planning. Engagement with DPC is sporadic.
levels.3: HoA engaged with DPC. Plans being formed. Digital beginning to appear in BP and team conversations.
levels.4: HoA has a clear digital plan for their area. Actively drives it in meetings. Engages consistently with DPC.
levels.5: HoA is a digital champion. Leads from the front. Digital is embedded in strategic planning and team culture.

infrastructureDevices.label: Infrastructure & Devices
subCriteria: Device availability and reliability for staff
Device availability and reliability for learners
Network / WiFi quality in teaching spaces
Access to specialist or industry software
IT support responsiveness

levels.1: Critical infrastructure failures. Devices unreliable or absent. WiFi inadequate. Teaching regularly disrupted.
levels.2: Significant device or connectivity gaps. Some specialist software inaccessible. IT issues unresolved.
levels.3: Adequate baseline. Some gaps in device numbers or specialist software. WiFi mostly functional.
levels.4: Good provision. Most staff and learners have reliable access. Minor gaps being addressed.
levels.5: Excellent provision. Reliable devices, fast connectivity, specialist software fully accessible. IT responsive.

digitalSkillsAssessment.label: Digital Skills Assessment & Tracking
subCriteria: Learner digital skills baseline on entry
Ongoing tracking of digital skills progress
Use of Century or equivalent tool
Digital skills mapped to framework (EDS/DigComp)
Evidence of progress used to adapt teaching

levels.1: No assessment or tracking of learner digital skills. No framework in use.
levels.2: Some baseline activity but no ongoing tracking. Framework awareness low. Century not deployed.
levels.3: Baseline exists. Some tracking in place. Century or equivalent partially deployed. Framework referenced.
levels.4: Regular baseline and tracking. Century or equivalent actively used. Digital skills mapped to EDS.
levels.5: Systematic assessment and tracking. Full Century IA use. Progress data used to adapt delivery. Framework embedded.

curriculumIntegration.label: Digital Curriculum Integration
subCriteria: Digital skills explicitly in scheme of learning
Digital tools embedded in classroom delivery
Industry-relevant digital tools in use
Assessment and feedback using digital tools
Digital practice documented in business plan

levels.1: Digital skills absent from curriculum planning. Delivery is primarily non-digital. No BP reference.
levels.2: Digital skills mentioned in plan but not embedded in delivery. Tools used inconsistently.
levels.3: Some digital tools in use. Referenced in BP. Industry tools beginning to appear. Not yet systematic.
levels.4: Digital embedded in most delivery. Industry tools actively used. BP references digital. Assessment mostly digital.
levels.5: Digital is fully integrated. Industry-standard tools explicit in curriculum. Assessment and feedback fully digital.

learnerReadiness.label: Learner Readiness & Digital Confidence
subCriteria: Learner device access at home
Learner confidence with required digital tools
Digital poverty mitigation in place
Learner engagement with digital learning activities
Learner digital independence demonstrated

levels.1: Significant digital poverty. Many learners unable to access digital learning outside college. Very low confidence.
levels.2: Digital poverty affects a minority significantly. Learner confidence low across team. Little mitigation.
levels.3: Some learners face access or confidence barriers. Area is aware. Mitigation partial.
levels.4: Most learners have access and basic confidence. Digital poverty addressed. Engagement generally good.
levels.5: Learners are digitally independent. Access issues minimal and mitigated. Strong digital engagement and confidence.

accessibilityHealth.label: Digital Health (Accessibility & Inclusion)
subCriteria: Staff awareness of accessibility requirements
Accessible resource design in practice
AT tools promoted and available to learners
GDPR and file/folder permissions practice
Teams environment structured for inclusive access

levels.1: No accessibility awareness. Resources not accessible. AT unknown. GDPR not observed in practice.
levels.2: Some awareness but not translated into practice. AT available but not promoted. GDPR patchy.
levels.3: Growing awareness. Some resources accessible. AT mentioned. GDPR understood but not consistently practised.
levels.4: Good practice observable. Most resources accessible. AT promoted. GDPR observed. Teams structured well.
levels.5: Accessibility by design is routine. Resources are fully accessible. AT embedded. GDPR excellent. Teams exemplary.

digitalLeadEngagement.label: Digital Lead Engagement & Health Checks
subCriteria: Digital Lead identified and confirmed
Digital Lead engaged with DPC programme
Digital Lead active in their area
Health Checks completed or planned
Digital Lead attending network events

levels.1: No Digital Lead identified. No Health Checks. No engagement with DL programme.
levels.2: Digital Lead identified but not yet active. Health Checks not started. Programme engagement minimal.
levels.3: Digital Lead confirmed. Beginning to engage. Health Checks planned. Attending some network events.
levels.4: Digital Lead active and engaged. Health Checks underway. Area developing with DL support.
levels.5: Digital Lead is proactive and independent. Health Checks embedded. Mentoring others. Strong network presence.
